metrics.name: Service level
metrics.description: Fraction of completed demand outcomes that reached the customer instead of being lost or cancelled.

metrics.name: Customer pressure
metrics.description: Total orders currently waiting either as open orders or backorders.

metrics.name: Stock position
metrics.description: Finished goods inventory minus customer backorders.

metrics.name: Inbound pipeline
metrics.description: Number of raw-material shipments currently in transit from both suppliers.

metrics.name: Average inbound risk
metrics.description: Mean risk score of shipments currently in the inbound pipeline.

metrics.name: Factory available
metrics.description: 1 when the production machine is up, 0 when it is down.

metrics.name: Scrap fraction
metrics.description: Share of completed production batches that failed quality inspection.

metrics.name: Suppliers down
metrics.description: How many of the two suppliers are currently disrupted.

metrics.name: Average waiting order age
metrics.description: Average age in days of all unfulfilled customer orders.

scenarios.name: Balanced dual source
scenarios.description: Baseline: moderate demand, split sourcing between reliable Supplier A and lower-cost Supplier B, normal factory reliability.

scenarios.name: Demand surge and port congestion
scenarios.description: High demand coincides with slower, riskier inbound logistics. Useful for exploring backlog and service-level collapse.

scenarios.name: Reliable supplier outage
scenarios.description: Supplier A starts disrupted, forcing the network toward cheaper but slower Supplier B until A recovers.

scenarios.name: Low-cost sourcing strategy
scenarios.description: Procurement leans into Supplier B. Costs fall conceptually, but longer lead times and higher risk stress service performance.

scenarios.name: Resilience investment
scenarios.description: Higher safety stock, more preventive maintenance, and faster repair/recovery rates reduce tail-risk events at the cost of extra buffer.
